logic: Dependency checking for CF7 versions (Mail Validation and Tag Generator).
Initialization of the plugin by hooking into 'plugins_loaded'.
Conditional display of admin notices if CF7 dependencies are not met.
Adding custom form tags to CF7 via 'wpcf7_init' hook.
Filtering hidden fields to inject the plugin version.
Configuration of various dynamic field types (text, hidden, email, url, tel, number, range, textarea, select, checkbox, radio, date, submit, label) in `wpcf7dtx_config`.
Registering and localizing the frontend JavaScript asset (`wpcf7dtx.js`) on the frontend.
Handling shortcode rendering for various field types (`wpcf7dtx_shortcode_handler`, `wpcf7dtx_button_shortcode_handler`, `wpcf7dtx_label_shortcode_handler`).
Evaluating dynamic values using `wpcf7dtx_get_dynamic` within handlers.
Applying and sanitizing various HTML attributes (e.g., `placeholder`, `value`, `aria-*`, `required`, `autofocus`, `disabled`, `readonly`, `data-dtx-value`).
Specialized attribute handling for select/checkbox/radio fields (options, default, size, etc.).
Specialized attribute handling for text fields (autocapitalize, autocomplete, maxlength, minlength, cols, rows, size, step).
Validation checks for attributes (e.g., min/max length comparison, valid autocomplete values).
Enqueuing scripts conditionally based on whether the asset is already registered.
AJAX handler (`wpcf7dtx_js_handler`) to process shortcodes dynamically after page load, verifying nonce and returning JSON array of evaluated values.
Inclusion of utility, validation, admin, and shortcode definition files.
Support for user-defined `dtx.php` files in theme/site root.
Initialization on document ready: Calls dtx.init()
Input selection: Selects all elements with class 'dtx-pageload' and attribute 'data-dtx-value'.
Shortcode parsing: Decodes 'data-dtx-value' (URL encoded), splits it into tag and attributes.
Value determination (Switch case):
- 'CF7_GET': Calls dtx.get(atts) to retrieve value from URL query.
- 'CF7_referrer': Calls dtx.referrer(atts) to retrieve referrer URL.
- 'CF7_URL': Calls dtx.current_url(atts) to retrieve current URL.
- 'CF7_get_cookie': Calls dtx.get_cookie(atts) to retrieve cookie value.
- 'CF7_guid': Calls dtx.guid() to generate a GUID.
- 'CF7_get_current_var': Checks for 'key' attribute; if 'url', calls dtx.current_url(atts); otherwise, skips (uses server value).
- 'CF7_get_post_var', 'CF7_get_custom_field', 'CF7_get_taxonomy', 'CF7_get_attachment', 'CF7_bloginfo', 'CF7_get_theme_option': Skips (assumes server value is correct).
- Default: Queues the shortcode request into dtx.queue for AJAX processing.
Value setting: If a value is determined synchronously, calls dtx.set($input, value).
AJAX Queue Processing: If dtx.queue is not empty, it schedules an AJAX call after 10ms.
AJAX Call: Sends queued shortcodes to `dtx_obj.ajax_url` with action 'wpcf7dtx'.
AJAX Success: Iterates through returned JSON data. For each item, it finds matching inputs, adds 'dtx-ajax-loaded' class, and calls dtx.set() to apply the value.
dtx.set() logic: Sets the 'value' attribute, adds 'dtx-loaded' class, and triggers 'dtx_init' event on the input.
dtx.get() logic: Uses URLSearchParams on window.location.search to fetch the value based on the 'key' attribute.
dtx.current_url() logic: Checks for 'part' attribute and returns specific parts (scheme, host, port, path, query, fragment) or the full URL.
dtx.guid() logic: Prefers `window.crypto.getRandomValues` for secure GUID generation; falls back to Math.random() combined with timestamp/performance.now() if crypto is unavailable.
Initializes the dtx object and sets up event listeners/logic upon page load.
Iterates over elements with class 'dtx-pageload' and attribute 'data-dtx-value' to queue up data retrieval tasks.
Handles various data retrieval methods based on the first element of the decoded data-dtx-value (e.g., 'CF7_GET', 'CF7_referrer', 'CF7_URL', etc.).
If a specific method is not matched, the data is pushed to the dtx.queue.
After initialization, if the queue is not empty, it triggers an AJAX call to send the queued data to the server.
The AJAX success callback iterates over the received data and updates the corresponding input field's value and adds the 'dtx-ajax-loaded' class.
dtx.validKey checks if a key exists in an object and is a non-empty string.
dtx.obfuscate converts a string into an HTML entity encoded string, optionally checking for an 'obfuscate' key in the provided object.
dtx.set updates an element's value, adds the 'dtx-loaded' class, and triggers the 'dtx_init' event.
dtx.get retrieves data from the URL query string, applying obfuscation if a key is provided.
dtx.referrer retrieves the document's referrer URL, applying obfuscation.
dtx.current_url retrieves parts of the current URL (scheme, host, port, path, query, fragment), applying obfuscation.
dtx.get_cookie retrieves a specific cookie value, applying obfuscation.
dtx.guid generates a unique identifier (GUID) using Web Crypto API if available, otherwise using Math.random() and Date/Performance API.
Initial class addition to DTX buttons within #tag-generator-list
Initial class addition to close-dialog buttons within .tag-generator-dialog
Definition of wpcf7dtx_taggen object

replaceAll function: Checks if input is a non-empty string and contains the search string (f). If so, it replaces all occurrences of f with r in input, handling regex escaping. If no_escape is true, it uses the string f directly as the regex.

updateOption function: Gets the current target element ($this), encodes its value after replacing single quotes (') with HTML entity '''. It then sets this encoded value to the sibling hidden input with data-tag-part='option' and triggers a 'change' event on it.
Initializes by selecting all buttons within elements matching '#tag-generator-list button[data-target*="tag-generator-panel-dynamic_"]' and adds the class 'dtx-form-tag' to them.
Adds the class 'button button-secondary' to all buttons within elements matching '.tag-generator-dialog button[data-taggen="close-dialog"]'.
The 'replaceAll' function replaces all occurrences of a substring (a) within a string (t) with another string (e). It handles optional global replacement (n).
The 'updateOption' function is triggered on change, keyup, or click events on inputs with the attribute 'data-tag-part="option"' inside a form with class 'tag-generator-panel'.
Inside 'updateOption', it retrieves the value from the current target element (t.val()).
It then calls 'replaceAll' to escape single quotes in the value and encode the result using encodeURIComponent.
Finally, it sets this encoded value to the sibling input element that has the attribute 'data-tag-part="option"' and triggers a 'change' event on that sibling.
Singleton pattern implementation for TagGenerator class.
Dependency check using `wpcf7dtx_dependencies` to ensure CF7 version >= 6.0.
Asset loading logic in `load_assets` based on admin hook and form page context.
Initialization logic in `init` to iterate over `wpcf7dtx_config()` and register tag generators.
Input attribute defaulting and merging in `default_input_atts`.
Comprehensive definition of allowed HTML tags and their attributes in `allowed_tags` (for 'control-box', 'header', 'footer').
Input generation logic in `input` handling datalist creation/reference, and special logic for 'option' attributes (checkbox/radio value handling).
Field wrapper generation in `field` which constructs the `<fieldset>` and `<legend>` structure.
Specific rendering methods (`text`, `checkbox`) that call `field` and `input` with predefined parameters.
URL generation in `doc_link` incorporating UTM parameters.
Main rendering logic in `render` which orchestrates the display of the entire tag generator interface based on the form tag type (`$tag_type`).

Conditional rendering of various input fields (e.g., 'required', 'field_name', 'value', 'default', 'placeholder', 'min', 'max', 'minlength', 'maxlength', 'autocomplete', 'readonly', 'dtx_pageload', 'akismet-*') based on `$tag_type`.
Specialized rendering blocks for 'select', 'checkbox', 'radio', and 'label' types to include extra options (e.g., 'multiple', 'include_blank', 'label_first', 'dtx_hide_blank').
Initialization of shortcodes by hooking into 'init' action.
wpcf7dtx_get: Retrieves variables from the $_GET array, applying sanitization, escaping, and filtering.
wpcf7dtx_post: Retrieves variables from the $_POST array, applying double sanitization, escaping, and filtering.
wpcf7dtx_url: Retrieves the current URL (or a specific part like scheme, host, path, query). Handles multisite/subdomain logic and applies filtering.
wpcf7dtx_referrer: Retrieves the HTTP_REFERER value from $_SERVER, applying sanitization, escaping, and filtering.
wpcf7dtx_bloginfo: Retrieves blog information (name, etc.) using get_bloginfo(), applying filtering.
wpcf7dtx_get_post_var: Retrieves a specific field (title, ID, slug) from a post using get_post_field(), handling aliases, and applying filtering.
wpcf7dtx_get_custom_field: Retrieves a custom field value from a post meta using get_post_meta(), checks key access permissions, and applies filtering.

wpcf7dtx_get_current_var: Retrieves dynamic variables based on the current object type (user, post, term, archive, search/404). It delegates to other functions (like wpcf7dtx_url, wpcf7dtx_get_attachment, wpcf7dtx_get_post_var, wpcf7dtx_get_taxonomy) based on the context.
wpcf7dtx_get_current_user: Retrieves data from the currently logged-in user using wp_get_current_user(), checks user data access permissions, and applies filtering.
wpcf7dtx_get_attachment: Retrieves attachment data (ID or URL) based on provided ID, post_id, size, and return type. Handles fetching featured image if ID is missing.
wpcf7dtx_get_cookie: Retrieves a specific cookie value from $_COOKIE, applying sanitization, escaping, and filtering.
wpcf7dtx_get_taxonomy: Retrieves a list of taxonomy terms (names, slugs, or IDs) associated with a post, joining them into a comma-separated string, and applying filtering.
wpcf7dtx_get_theme_option: Retrieves a theme modification value using get_theme_mod(), falling back to a default value if the key is not found, and applying filtering.
wpcf7dtx_guid: Generates a random GUID (Globally Unique Identifier) using com_create_guid() or mt_rand(), and applies filtering.
wpcf7dtx_allow_protocols: Handles protocol filtering (defaulting to http/https, allowing replacement or merging).
wpcf7dtx_sanitize: Sanitizes input based on detected or specified type (email, url, key, slug, textarea).
wpcf7dtx_escape: Escapes output based on detected or specified type, optionally obfuscating it.
wpcf7dtx_detect_value_type: Determines if a string is a 'url', 'email', or defaults to 'text'.
wpcf7dtx_obfuscate: Converts a string into an HTML entity representation (e.g., 'a' becomes '&#97;').
wpcf7dtx_validate_post_id: Checks if the current user has permission to view a given post ID.
wpcf7dtx_user_can_view_post: Determines viewability based on public status, password requirement, or user capabilities.
wpcf7dtx_get_post_id: Retrieves the post ID, prioritizing input, global $post, or the loop, handling AJAX context.
wpcf7dtx_get_dynamic: Retrieves and sanitizes dynamic content from a WPCF7_FormTag object, supporting shortcode evaluation.
wpcf7dtx_get_dynamic_attr: Retrieves dynamic attributes from a WPCF7_FormTag, specialized for handling CSS classes.
wpcf7dtx_dynamic_attr: Wrapper function to fetch and sanitize dynamic attribute values.
wpcf7dtx_get_allowed_field_properties: Returns an array of allowed HTML attributes based on the input field type.
wpcf7dtx_format_atts: Converts an associative array of attributes into a space-separated HTML attribute string, handling boolean and class attributes.
wpcf7dtx_input_html: Generates the basic HTML input tag.
wpcf7dtx_checkbox_html: Generates HTML for a single checkbox/radio button, handling label wrapping and default state.
wpcf7dtx_checkbox_group_html: Generates HTML for a group of checkboxes/radios, handling dynamic options, JSON parsing, and class assignment.
wpcf7dtx_textarea_html: Generates the HTML textarea tag.
wpcf7dtx_options_html: Generates HTML for `<option>` tags from an array of key/value pairs.
wpcf7dtx_select_html: Generates the full `<select>` tag, handling options, option groups, placeholders, and default selection.
wpcf7dtx_array_has_key: Safely checks if a key exists in an array and has a truthy/numeric/boolean value.
wpcf7dtx_post_meta_key_access_is_allowed: Checks if a specific post meta key is allowed based on global settings (allow all or explicit list).
wpcf7dtx_user_data_access_is_allowed: Checks if a specific user data key is allowed based on global settings (allow all or explicit list).
wpcf7dtx_parse_allowed_keys: Parses a newline-separated string of keys into a PHP array.
wpcf7dtx_split_newlines: Splits a string by various newline characters (CRLF, CR, LF).
wpcf7dtx_get_settings: Retrieves the main DTX settings array from the database.
wpcf7dtx_update_settings: Saves the DTX settings array to the database.
wpcf7dtx_access_denied_alert: Triggers a PHP warning when a dynamic key is accessed but is not in the allowed list.
wpcf7dtx_messages: Ajoute des messages de validation frontend personnalisés en fusionnant avec les messages existants de Contact Form 7.
wpcf7dtx_config_validator_available_error_codes: Ajoute le code d'erreur 'dtx_disallowed' aux codes d'erreur disponibles du validateur de configuration CF7.

wpcf7dtx_validation_filter: Filtre la validation des champs de formulaire DTX. Il gère les valeurs simples, les tableaux de valeurs sélectionnables (single/multiple), et appelle wpcf7dtx_validate_value pour la validation détaillée.
wpcf7dtx_validate_value: Valide une valeur donnée en fonction de son type (email, tel, number, date). Vérifie également les règles de présence (required) et les contraintes de longueur (min/max).

wpcf7dtx_validate: Valide les tags de formulaire dans le contexte de la configuration (backend). Il parcourt tous les tags, vérifie les valeurs par défaut et les placeholders pour détecter les données sensibles (via wpcf7dtx_validate_sensitive_value), et gère les erreurs de syntaxe d'email dans les configurations de mail.
wpcf7dtx_init_validator: Initialise le validateur en ajoutant wpcf7dtx_validate au hook 'wpcf7_config_validator_validate' après le chargement des plugins.

wpcf7dtx_validate_sensitive_value: Valide si une chaîne de contenu (souvent un shortcode) tente d'accéder à des données sensibles. Il utilise shortcode_parse_atts pour identifier le shortcode et la clé, puis appelle des fonctions spécifiques (wpcf7dtx_post_meta_key_access_is_allowed ou wpcf7dtx_user_data_access_is_allowed) pour déterminer si l'accès est autorisé.

wpcf7dtx_access_denied_alert: Triggers a PHP warning when a dynamic key is accessed but is not in the allowed list.
wpcf7dtx_messages: Ajoute des messages de validation frontend personnalisés en fusionnant avec les messages existants de Contact Form 7.
wpcf7dtx_config_validator_available_error_codes: Ajoute le code d'erreur 'dtx_disallowed' aux codes d'erreur disponibles du validateur de configuration CF7.

wpcf7dtx_validation_filter: Filtre la validation des champs de formulaire DTX. Il gère les valeurs simples, les tableaux de valeurs sélectionnables (single/multiple), et appelle wpcf7dtx_validate_value pour la validation détaillée.
wpcf7dtx_validate_value: Valide une valeur donnée en fonction de son type (email, tel, number, date). Vérifie également les règles de présence (required) et les contraintes de longueur (min/max).

wpcf7dtx_validate: Valide les tags de formulaire dans le contexte de la configuration (backend). Il parcourt tous les tags, vérifie les valeurs par défaut et les placeholders pour détecter les données sensibles (via wpcf7dtx_validate_sensitive_value), et gère les erreurs de syntaxe d'email dans les configurations de mail.
wpcf7dtx_init_validator: Initialise le validateur en ajoutant wpcf7dtx_validate au hook 'wpcf7_config_validator_validate' après le chargement des plugins.

wpcf7dtx_validate_sensitive_value: Valide si une chaîne de contenu (souvent un shortcode) tente d'accéder à des données sensibles. Il utilise shortcode_parse_atts pour identifier le shortcode et la clé, puis appelle des fonctions spécifiques (wpcf7dtx_post_meta_key_access_is_allowed ou wpcf7dtx_user_data_access_is_allowed) pour déterminer si l'accès est autorisé.
